title: About
content:
# About Stout Heart Press# Stout Heart Press has emerged out of a deep appreciation for the analog and for the community of printers who have welcomed me into the craft.# 2023# At some point during the Summer of 2023 I bought a pack of pink pearl erasers to play with a vintage linocut tool from the thrift store. With some watercolor paper and a black stamp pad, I began making cards with repeating patterns.
I made my first press - like a giant tortilla press, made with two Ikea cutting boards and a few hinges. It worked surprisingly well for a few linocuts, but eventually the lever I made wore out.
2024# I began my Library Science graduate program at the University of Michigan in the Fall of 2024, at which point I discovered the Book Arts Studio on campus. I began working with moveable type and large proofing presses, learning from Ann Arbor printer and wood engraver, Jim Horton.
2025# By the middle of my graduate program, book binding, letterpress, and linocut printing became ways of taking care of myself. I loved working with printmaking-centered collections in the archives I worked in on campus.
2026# Over the past year I have spent as much time as possible working in the studio, have taught several letterpress classes and tutored individual students working on book binding projects.

title: Work
content: This is where I talk about the work I’ve made#
